note: Final adoption decision (retain v4 / revert to v3) is left to the coordinator/user; this worker presents statistical facts only.

evalCliSha256.note: rebuilt via cargo build --release -p engine --bin eval_cli at current HEAD before comparing

engineSourceSha256.note: confirmed via v3 run's own meta, built at the same current HEAD

protocolParameters.note: engine_depth=12, engine_exact_from_empties=16, engine_time_ms=1500, engine_max_nodes=160000, engine_exact_quota_percent=60, unlimited_exact_empties=20, engine_tt_mb=64, engine_modes=[single-root], levels=[10], opening_set=primary, opening_count=30 -- identical in both settings dicts

v4Reuse.conclusion: All provenance fields match exactly, including the freshly rebuilt eval_cli binary SHA-256. v4's 60 games from T158d stage 2 are reused verbatim; only v3's 60 games were newly executed.

protocol.sourceOfTruth: identical to T158d / T125 production adoption gate; vs_edax.py unmodified from its T158d state

historicalT121T125V3Reference.note: informational cross-check only; win/draw/loss counts match exactly, mean margin differs by only 0.033 discs; engine binary has evolved since T121 (T139 symmetric-position ordering fix, T156 MPC additions, T158a eval features), consistent with the small drift

openingLevel.diffDefinition: v3 opening-average margin minus v4 opening-average margin

bootstrap.method: paired bootstrap percentile CI, identical algorithm to bench/edax-compare/compare_pattern_v3.py#paired_bootstrap and to T158d's stage-2 report (random.Random(seed), resample-with-replacement len(diffs) draws per sample via rng.choice, mean each sample, sort, percentile 2.5/97.5)

gameLevel.note: supplementary finer-grained view; mean matches opening-level exactly by linearity

comparisonWithT158d.note: v3-v4 point estimate is larger than candidate-v4, but both CIs are wide and overlap substantially; the low statistical power of this 30-opening/60-game protocol (already documented in T125's limitations section) means no clear ranking among v3/v4/candidate can be established from this experiment alone.

exactFallbackDefinition.note: Per T158d verifier follow-up request, the exactFallback tally counts ONLY the single transition move (the last budgeted-phase move before the engine's root empties drops to <= unlimited_exact_empties=20), checking that move's exactFallback flag. It does NOT count exactFallback occurrences on any other budgeted-phase move earlier in the game.

budgetedToExactDeviation.description: Deviation between the last budgeted-phase discDiff (empties ~21-22) and the first unlimited-exact discDiff (empties <=20), across all v3 and v4 games where both phases were reached

excludedGames.reason: engine (v3, black) was completely wiped out (0 discs) and had no legal moves before the game ever reached the unlimited-exact (empties<=20) phase; game terminated at empties=19 per the harness's no-legal-move termination rule. Legitimate game outcome, not a crash/illegal-move/anomaly.

budgetedToExactDeviation.interpretation: v3's mean deviation is somewhat larger than v4's (6.17 vs 5.14), but v3's max (15.78) is well below v4's max (32.44, already established in T158d). Consistent with T158d's conclusion that pilot-stage asymmetries can be small-sample artifacts; here the difference is modest and not alarming.

signFlips.description: Games where budgeted-phase discDiff sign differs from the first unlimited-exact discDiff sign
signFlips.conclusion: similar counts (5/59 vs 4/60); no v3-specific anomaly. All flips originate from small near-zero budgeted values, no catastrophic reversal.

anomalyCheck.earlyTerminationNote: v3 game primary-30/black ended at empties=19 due to legitimate complete elimination (0 discs, no legal moves), not an anomaly

outOfScope: final adoption decision (retain v4 / revert to v3)
